body.body: The ebook reader (ebook_reader.py + reading_integrator.py) is currently Python. Re-express it entirely as instantiated templates: FILE_ITERATOR over the reading queue, READER_LOOP per book, MEMORY_DEPOSIT per extracted concept, SEARCH_AND_RESPOND for dedup check. Output: a seed script that produces all the habits needed to run the reader without touching the Python reader code. Validation: habit-program reader produces equivalent nodes to Python reader on same input. This is the proof of concept that the matrix language is complete enough for real programs. Prereq: T-template-seed-patterns.
body.title: Re-express Python reader as a habit program — first complete matrix-language program
body.result: seed_reader_habits.py: 4 Engram template habits instantiated from tpl-file-iterator/reader-loop/search-and-respond/memory-deposit. Nodes in Postgres: proc_file_iter_reader_queue (FILE_ITERATOR → open_book), proc_reader_ebook (READER_LOOP → read_chunk), proc_sar_reading_concept_extracted (SEARCH_AND_RESPOND → cortex.search), proc_deposit_reading_concept (MEMORY_DEPOSIT → _reading_extract_worker). Python code untouched. Inter-habit chaining deferred to T-trails-infra. First complete matrix-language program proven. Tests 110/110.
